reasoning: The government has explicitly stated (as recently as May 6, 2025) that it will not initiate an independence referendum itself, leaving it entirely to a citizen-led petition process under the newly introduced Bill 54. The process requires gathering ~177,000 signatures over a 120-day period. Because Bill 54 is still moving through the legislature and the 120-day signature collection window extends well past the July 1, 2025 deadline, it is virtually impossible for the petition to conclude, be verified, and have a definitive date officially scheduled by July 1. An official scheduling usually requires an Order in Council or a similarly formal binding decree, which will not occur before the triggering condition (the petition) is met. Given the tight 54-day window to July 1, 2025, the probability is extremely low.
